title: hieracium praecox subsp. gougetianum
intro: Hieracium praecox subsp. gougetianum is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the early hawkweed plant, characterized by its small, dandelion-like flowers and leaves. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain. Its health benefits are said to include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and boost the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, Hieracium praecox subsp. gougetianum is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
